channel_description: Get quick, clear summaries for every Hacker News story—no more info overload. This bot reads each post and gives you the key points in seconds. Perfect for staying updated without reading full threads.

posts.text:
Incident with Multple GitHub Services

🔸GitHub's Status Page - Incident with multple GitHub services.

🌍 @hackernews_summary

A DIY Watch You Can Actually Wear

🔸The LILYGO T-Watch Ultra is a DIY smartwatch with ESP32-S3, IP65-rated case, and various features like AI acceleration, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and LoRa. It's suitable for complex applications and has a long runtime due to its 1,100mAh battery and improved display.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

If America's So Rich, How'd It Get So Sad?

🔸The US has experienced a sharp decline in self-reported happiness since 2020, with no recovery, despite a strong economy and low unemployment. The culprit is likely the ongoing COVID pandemic and its aftermath, including inflation, economic uncertainty, and a uniquely negative news environment.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

France confirms data breach at government agency that manages citizens' IDs

🔸A data breach at the Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (ANTS) exposed personal info of an undisclosed number of citizens. Millions may have been affected, with a hacker claiming to have 19 million records on a hacking forum.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

The end of responsive images

🔸The author of the article, a former Chair of the RICG, confesses to creating the responsive image syntax that has caused frustration for developers. They reveal that they hated the syntax but fought for it because they knew developers would have done it wrong.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

To Protect and Swerve: NYPD Cop Has 547 Speeding Tickets

🔸James Giovansanti, a NYPD officer, has accumulated 547 speeding tickets in Staten Island since 2022, averaging one ticket every other day. His record makes him the second-most-reckless driver in the city, posing a unique danger to himself and others.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

A History of Erasures Learning to Write Like Leylâ Erbil

🔸The author initially dismissed Leylâ Erbil's work as outdated and self-indulgent, but later reevaluated her experimental novel "What Remains" as a powerful challenge to literary conventions in Turkey. Erbil's autofiction explores Turkish history, politics, and identity through a stream-of-consciousness narrative that blurs the lines between personal and public trauma.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Bitwarden CLI Compromised in Ongoing Checkmarx Supply Chain Campaign

🔸Bitwarden CLI 2026.4.0 was compromised through a GitHub Action in its CI/CD pipeline as part of the Checkmarx supply chain campaign. Users are advised to review CI logs and rotate exposed secrets.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Modern Board Games: and why you should play them (2022)

🔸The user presented a team with an introduction to modern board games, dispelling common misconceptions that there are only a few board games and that they are only for children. They highlighted the diversity and complexity of modern board games, encouraging the team to try them out for their social, intellectual, and entertaining benefits.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Trump administration reclassifies cannabis as less dangerous

🔸The US Department of Justice reclassified cannabis as less dangerous, moving it from Schedule I to Schedule III, allowing for increased research and access. The change is seen as symbolic, but could lead to policymakers considering full legalization.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

A Renaissance gambling dispute spawned probability theory

🔸Mathematicians Blaise Pascal and Pierre de Fermat solved the "problem of points" by considering future possibilities of the score. Their solution, based on expected value, is used in risk assessments and has become a fundamental pillar of modern probability theory.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Work with the Garage Door Up

🔸Creative people sharing their process can create invested followings and avoid pitching problems. This type of communication is like having a physical business sign, showing "I am here, working."

🌍 @hackernews_summary

Sneaky spam in conversational replies to blog posts

🔸A spammer used AI to post three fake comments on a blog, each pretending to be a reply to the others, with a suspicious link in the middle one. The comments were spaced 3 minutes apart and came from the same IP address in the Philippines.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Surveillance vendors caught abusing access to telcos to track people's locations

🔸Citizen Lab exposed two surveillance campaigns using "ghost" companies to exploit phone network vulnerabilities. The campaigns targeted individuals worldwide, using SS7 and Diameter flaws to geolocate phones.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Show HN: Honker – Postgres NOTIFY/LISTEN Semantics for SQLite

🔸Honker is a SQLite extension and language bindings that add durable pub/sub, task queue, and event streams to SQLite without client polling or a daemon/broker, allowing atomic business writes and side-effect enqueues. It achieves single-digit millisecond reaction time and supports various languages including Python, Node, Rust, Go, Ruby, Bun, and Elixir.

23 Apr 2026

💬 comments
🌍 @hackernews_summary

Email could have been X.400 times better

🔸Email's early history involved a complex system called X.400, which had features like recall and encryption, but was difficult to use and implement. SMTP, a simpler protocol, eventually became the standard for email, outliving X.400 due to its ease of use and rapid iteration.

23 Apr 2026

🌍 @hackernews_summary

Writing a C Compiler, in Zig

🔸This is a series of articles I wrote while writing paella, following Nora Sandler's Writing a C Compiler. It was both an exercise to learn Zig and a way to waste time instead of looking for work, as I am currently "between jobs". I did not edit them as I collect them here outside of fixing some broken links.

Do you want the US to "win" AI?

🔸The writer is concerned about the direction of AI development and its potential impact on society, preferring a more nuanced approach to technology. They question Elon's worldview and the value of a society where AI is controlled by powerful individuals.

🌍 @hackernews_summary

Highlights from Git 2.54

🔸Git 2.54 introduces new features including the experimental 'git history' command for rewriting commit history and a new way to define hooks in configuration files. Other notable changes include improved usability in 'git add -p' and 'git log -L', as well as enhancements to 'git blame' and 'git backfill'.
